articles.title: Chip Rebound Drives Stocks Higher as Oil Eases: Markets Wrap

articles.title: London Stock Exchange to Introduce Nonstop Trading Next Year

articles.title: Gilts Steady as Investors Await More Policy Details From Burnham
articles.summary: UK government bonds posted small moves as investors awaited fresh policy details from new Prime Minister Andy Burnham, while weak economic data dimmed bets on higher interest rates.

articles.title: Gold Rises on Dip-Buying as Traders Weigh Middle East Clashes
articles.summary: Gold touched its highest level in a week on dip-buying as traders monitored developments in the Middle East for clues on the energy price impact on inflation.

articles.title: Goldman Says Brent Could Top $120 If Hormuz Disruptions Persist
articles.summary: Brent could rally to more than $120 a barrel by the fourth quarter if disruptions of the Strait of Hormuz persist, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc., although that’s not the bank’s base case.
